name: Out of Africa
author: Karen Blixen
description: Blixen's lyrical memoir of her 17 years running a coffee plantation in colonial Kenya. One of the great works of 20th-century literature — made into an Oscar-winning film.

name: The Complete Fairy Tales
author: Hans Christian Andersen
description: The essential collection of Andersen's fairy tales — The Little Mermaid, The Snow Queen, Thumbelina, The Ugly Duckling and over 150 more. Foundational works of world literature.

name: Either/Or
author: Søren Kierkegaard
description: Kierkegaard's masterwork presenting two life philosophies: the aesthetic and the ethical. A foundational text of existentialist philosophy, written under the pseudonym Victor Eremita.

name: Miss Smilla's Feeling for Snow
author: Peter Høeg
description: A half-Danish half-Greenlandic woman investigates a child's mysterious death in Copenhagen. A thriller that is also a profound meditation on identity, belonging and Arctic ice.

name: The Danish Way of Parenting
author: Jessica Joelle Alexander
description: Explores how Danish parenting values — free play, authenticity, no ultimatums, empathy and togetherness — create some of the world's happiest and most resilient adults.

name: The Little Book of Hygge
author: Meik Wiking
description: The CEO of the Happiness Research Institute in Copenhagen explains the Danish concept of hygge — cosiness, togetherness and the art of creating happiness. A global bestseller.

name: Denmark: A History
author: Knud J.V. Jespersen
description: A comprehensive and accessible history of Denmark from the Viking Age to the modern welfare state. Essential reading for understanding Danish identity and society.

name: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo (Millennium series)
author: Stieg Larsson
description: Though Swedish, this globally influential Nordic noir series transformed Scandinavian crime fiction — included for its defining role in making Nordic crime a global phenomenon.
